channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Kane, England play Ghana at the World Cup: prediction, team news, lineups
item.description: Harry Kane's England and Ghana meet in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Group L, with the winner advancing to the round of 32.

item.title: Iran says technical talks with US in Switzerland conclude successfully
item.description: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Ghalibaf also says an agreement reached with the US to release $12bn in frozen assets.

item.title: Israel’s deliberate targeting of Gaza children part of genocide: UN inquiry
item.description: United Nations Commission of Inquiry says Israel's 'deliberate targeting' of Gaza children part of ongoing 'genocide'.

item.title: About 20 drown in France trying to escape heatwave sweeping much of Europe
item.description: France struggles with extreme heat and power grid challenges as temperature records also smashed in parts of UK, Spain.

item.title: Iran war day 116: US eases Iran sanctions; Lebanon ceasefire holds
item.description: US eases oil sanctions for 60 days after Iran agrees to allow international nuclear inspections.

item.title: US gov’t investigates New York coffee chain over ban on pro-Israel lawmaker
item.description: Top civil rights prosecutor announces probe after cafe chain says it will not serve Democratic lawmaker Dan Goldman.

item.title: What to know about Tuesday’s primary elections in Maryland, Utah, New York
item.description: Redistricting will be central to Democratic primaries in Utah and Maryland while South Carolina faces Republican run-off.

item.title: How a dead extremist Rabbi still shapes Israeli politics today
item.description: How a dead extremist Rabbi still shapes Israeli politics today

item.title: EU to hold migration meeting with Taliban officials in Brussels
item.description: The meeting is expected to focus on the deportation of Afghan nationals who do not have a right to stay in Europe.

item.title: Why Iran’s President Pezeshkian is heading to Pakistan after US talks
item.description: This is Pezeshkian's first overseas trip since Iran war began, and comes a day after the high-stakes Switzerland talks.

item.title: Ronaldo, Portugal play Uzbekistan in World Cup: prediction and team news
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal face Uzbekistan in a must-win group match, as race for the knockouts intensifies.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade: Bucks star to join Miami Heat – reports
item.description: The reported deal draws a line under months of speculation concerning the 10-time All-Star's future in Milwaukee.

item.title: Football offers escape for Gaza, but World Cup spirit dimmed
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza struggle to find World Cup joy amid displacement, power outages, and memories of previous lives.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israel’s ‘quiet annexation’ grows louder
item.description: Al Jazeera cameraman killed in Gaza, West Bank mosques torched, as Israeli officials describe attempt to expand control.

item.title: Algeria rally late to knock Jordan out of World Cup 2026
item.description: Goals from Nadhir Benbouali and Amine Gouiri steer Algeria to a dramatic second-half comeback in Group J.

item.title: Colombia’s leftist presidential candidate vows to challenge election result
item.description: Colombia’s leftist presidential candidate Ivan Cepeda has vowed to challenge the results of the presidential runoff.

item.title: Which teams have qualified for the World Cup 2026 knockouts, round of 32?
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage format, criteria and rules for qualification. Find out who's in and who's out.

item.title: Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem Pizzaballa makes solidarity visit to Gaza
item.description: Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem, Cardinal Pizzaballa visited Gaza in a show of solidarity with its Christian community.

item.title: Iraq fans ‘proud’ and joyful despite World Cup loss to France
item.description: Iraqis in Philadelphia weather Mbappe and a storm, but still find happiness in their team's FIFA World Cup performance.

item.title: Bullying investigated after rare Philippines school shooting kills three
item.description: Three students are killed and seven others wounded after a rare school shooting in the central Philippines.

item.title: Five Eyes intelligence alliance warns of threats from new AI models
item.description: Frontier AI models are 'fundamentally transforming' offensive cyber capabilities, intelligence officials say.

item.title: Last one, the best one? How Lionel Messi keeps doing it at the World Cup
item.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni, who has built an ecosystem around Messi, lauds the captain’s commitment to the team.

item.title: Conservative radio host defends Trump, says he’s better than Vance
item.description: Prominent conservative radio host, spoke at the International Policy Summit to defend Trump’s handling of the Iran war.

item.title: Alan Greenspan, former US Federal Reserve chairman, dies at 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan, one of the most influential economic policymakers in modern US history, has died aged 100.

item.title: Haaland scores two as Norway beat Senegal 3-2, enter World Cup knockouts
item.description: Senegal fought back until stoppage time but couldn’t draw level and must beat Iraq for a chance to progress.
